title: Follow Fitts' Law for Interactive Elements
description: Make clickable targets large enough to be easily hit. Minimum 44x44px for touch targets on mobile devices.

title: Use Progressive Disclosure
description: Reveal information progressively to avoid overwhelming users. Show only what is needed at each step of a task.

title: Maintain Consistent Navigation
description: Keep navigation in the same location across all pages. Users rely on muscle memory and spatial navigation.

title: Provide Clear Error Messages
description: Error messages must explain what went wrong and how to fix it. Avoid technical jargon in user-facing errors.

title: Implement Keyboard Navigation
description: All interactive elements must be accessible via keyboard. Tab order must follow logical visual flow.

title: Meet Color Contrast Requirements
description: Maintain minimum 4.5:1 contrast ratio for normal text and 3:1 for large text per WCAG 2.1 AA standards.

title: Show Loading States
description: Always indicate when content is loading. Use skeletons for layout-heavy content and spinners for actions.

title: Confirm Destructive Actions
description: Always ask for confirmation before irreversible actions like deleting or overwriting data.

title: Optimize First Contentful Paint
description: Target FCP under 1.8 seconds. Users perceive sites as slow if content doesn't appear quickly.

title: Use Semantic HTML Elements
description: Proper semantic HTML improves accessibility screen reader compatibility and SEO simultaneously.

title: Design for Thumb Zones on Mobile
description: Place primary actions in thumb-friendly zones. Critical buttons should not be in the top corners on mobile.

title: Prevent Form Submission Errors
description: Validate forms inline as users type. Don't wait until submission to reveal all errors at once.

title: Implement Focus Indicators
description: Visible focus indicators are required for all interactive elements. Never remove outline without a replacement.

title: Reduce Cognitive Load
description: Limit choices to 5-9 items per group. Break complex tasks into smaller digestible steps.

title: Use System Fonts for Performance
description: Consider using system font stack for body text to eliminate render-blocking font loads.

title: Implement Lazy Loading for Images
description: Defer offscreen image loading to improve initial page load time and reduce bandwidth.

title: Maintain Visual Hierarchy
description: Use size color and weight to establish clear hierarchy. The most important element should be most prominent.

title: Provide Undo Functionality
description: Allow users to undo recent actions. This reduces anxiety and encourages exploration.

title: Design Empty States
description: Always design empty states with helpful guidance. Empty states are an opportunity for onboarding.

title: Use Meaningful Animations
description: Animations should reinforce spatial relationships and provide context. Avoid purely decorative motion.

title: Implement Skip Navigation Links
description: Add skip links for screen reader users to bypass repetitive navigation blocks.

title: Optimize Touch Target Spacing
description: Ensure minimum 8px spacing between adjacent touch targets to prevent accidental taps.

title: Use Consistent Iconography
description: Icons should follow a consistent style size and stroke weight throughout the application.

title: Provide Alt Text for Images
description: All informational images must have descriptive alt text. Decorative images should have empty alt attributes.

title: Implement Responsive Typography
description: Use fluid typography that scales proportionally with viewport size using clamp() or viewport units.

title: Design for Offline States
description: Applications should handle network failures gracefully with appropriate offline states and retry mechanisms.

title: Use Consistent Spacing Scale
description: Establish and adhere to a spacing scale (4px or 8px base). Avoid arbitrary spacing values.

title: Implement Breadcrumb Navigation
description: Use breadcrumbs for complex hierarchical navigation to help users understand their location.

title: Avoid Dark Patterns
description: Never use deceptive UI patterns like hidden unsubscribe buttons or misleading default selections.

title: Write Microcopy That Guides
description: Button labels and helper text should describe outcomes not actions. Use "Save changes" not just "Submit".

title: Implement Pagination or Infinite Scroll
description: For long lists choose between pagination and infinite scroll based on use case. Pagination for searchable content.

title: Use Card Components Consistently
description: Cards should have consistent padding corner radius and shadow depth throughout the application.

title: Design Accessible Form Labels
description: All form inputs must have visible associated labels. Placeholder text is not a substitute for labels.

title: Optimize Critical Rendering Path
description: Inline critical CSS eliminate render-blocking resources and defer non-critical scripts.

title: Use Motion to Provide Context
description: Use spatial transitions that match the mental model. Drill-down should slide left; going back should slide right.

title: Implement Search with Filters
description: Complex datasets need search combined with filters. Allow users to narrow down large result sets efficiently.

title: Design Notification Systems
description: Notifications should be timely relevant and dismissible. Use appropriate urgency levels (toast info warning error).

title: Use Clear Call-to-Action Hierarchy
description: Each page or screen should have one primary CTA. Secondary actions should be visually subordinate.

title: Implement Session Persistence
description: Save user state and preferences across sessions. Don't make users re-configure settings on every visit.

title: Design for Right-to-Left Languages
description: If supporting RTL languages ensure layouts icons and animations all support bidirectional text.

title: Use Predictive Search
description: Implement autocomplete for search fields. Reduce user effort by predicting queries from the first few characters.

title: Maintain 60fps Animations
description: Animations should target 60fps. Use CSS transforms and opacity for best performance. Avoid animating layout properties.

title: Implement Role-Based Access
description: UI should reflect the user's permissions. Don't show controls the user cannot use; hide or disable them contextually.

title: Design Accessible Color Communication
description: Never use color alone to convey information. Always pair with text icons or patterns for color-blind users.

title: Use Skeleton Screens for Loading
description: Skeleton screens reduce perceived load time and prevent layout shift when content loads.

title: Implement Deep Linking
description: All application states should be bookmarkable via URL. Users should be able to share and return to any state.

title: Design for Variable Content
description: UI components must accommodate varying content lengths. Test with minimum maximum and typical content.

title: Use Autofill-Friendly Form Attributes
description: Add correct autocomplete attributes to form fields to enable browser autofill for faster form completion.

title: Implement Gesture Navigation
description: Support common gestures (swipe pull-to-refresh) on touch devices where they add value.

title: Design Accessible Tables
description: Data tables need proper th scope headers and caption elements for screen reader compatibility.

title: Use Anchor Links for Long Pages
description: Long content pages should use jump navigation links to help users navigate to specific sections quickly.

title: Avoid Infinite Horizontal Scroll
description: Horizontal scroll is unexpected and hard to use except in specific contexts like image galleries or carousels.

title: Implement Exit Intent Handling
description: For forms or multi-step processes warn users before losing unsaved work with browser beforeunload events.

title: Optimize Time to Interactive
description: TTI should be under 3.8 seconds. Defer non-critical JavaScript and use code splitting.

title: Design Responsive Data Tables
description: Data tables on mobile should adapt with horizontal scroll column hiding or card-based views.

title: Use Consistent Button Styles
description: Define primary secondary and tertiary button styles. Consistently apply them throughout the application.

title: Implement Contextual Help
description: Provide in-context tooltips help icons and documentation links near complex features.

title: Design for Touch Hover States
description: On touch devices hover states should not be relied upon. Ensure all hover-only content has a touch-accessible equivalent.

title: Use Appropriate Link Colors
description: Links should be visually distinguishable from surrounding text without relying solely on color.

title: Implement Caching Strategies
description: Use appropriate caching for static assets API responses and user data to improve repeat visit performance.

title: Design Accessible Modals
description: Modal dialogs must trap focus manage keyboard navigation and return focus to trigger element on close.

title: Avoid Auto-Playing Media
description: Never autoplay video or audio content with sound. Users find unexpected audio intrusive and disorienting.

title: Use Data Visualization Best Practices
description: Choose chart types appropriate for data relationships. Use clear labels appropriate scales and accessible colors.

title: Implement Print Stylesheets
description: Provide print-friendly styles that remove navigation ads and unnecessary UI chrome for printing.

title: Design Effective Onboarding Flows
description: First-time user onboarding should be contextual minimal and focused on delivering core value quickly.

title: Use Semantic Versioning for APIs
description: API endpoints should use versioning to prevent breaking changes for existing consumers.

title: Optimize Web Font Loading
description: Use font-display: swap to prevent invisible text during font loading. Preload critical font files.

title: Design Accessible Custom Controls
description: Custom UI controls (dropdowns sliders toggles) must fully replicate native accessibility behavior.

title: Implement CSP Headers
description: Content Security Policy headers protect against XSS attacks and should be implemented on all applications.

title: Use Appropriate Whitespace
description: Generous whitespace reduces cognitive load and increases readability. Don't compress designs excessively.

title: Design Mobile-First Layouts
description: Build layouts starting from the smallest viewport and enhance for larger screens.

title: Implement Accessible Carousels
description: Carousels and sliders need pause controls keyboard navigation and appropriate ARIA labels.

title: Use Clear Page Titles
description: Each page must have a unique descriptive title in the browser tab for navigation and SEO.

title: Design for Low Bandwidth
description: Optimize assets and implement progressive loading for users on slow network connections.

title: Implement Status Pages
description: System status pages build trust. Show real-time service health uptime metrics and incident history.

title: Use Logical Focus Order
description: Tab focus order must follow the visual and logical reading order of the interface.

title: Avoid Overusing Modals
description: Modals interrupt workflow. Use inline editing expandable sections or sidepanels as less disruptive alternatives.

title: Design Consistent Alert Styles
description: Success warning error and info alerts should use consistent colors icons and positioning.

title: Implement ARIA Landmarks
description: Use ARIA landmark roles (main nav aside header footer) to give screen reader users quick navigation.

title: Test with Real User Data
description: Use production-like data in testing. Edge cases (long names empty states special characters) reveal critical bugs.

title: Design for One-Handed Mobile Use
description: Critical actions and navigation should be reachable with one hand. Consider bottom navigation patterns.

title: Implement Localization Support
description: Build i18n into the architecture from the start. Retrofitting localization is costly and error-prone.

title: Use Consistent Terminology
description: Use the same words for the same concepts throughout the UI. Inconsistent vocabulary creates confusion.

title: Reduce Motion for Accessibility
description: Respect prefers-reduced-motion media query. Provide non-animated alternatives for users with vestibular disorders.

title: Implement Content Security Headers
description: Add security headers (X-Frame-Options HSTS X-Content-Type-Options) to all web applications.

title: Design Accessible Tooltips
description: Tooltips must be accessible via keyboard hover and must not contain critical information unavailable elsewhere.

title: Use Data Tables for Tabular Data
description: Data structured in rows and columns belongs in a table not a visual grid built with divs.

title: Optimize LCP Performance
description: Largest Contentful Paint should be under 2.5 seconds. Optimize hero images and critical resources.

title: Design Accessible Dropdowns
description: Dropdown menus need proper ARIA roles keyboard navigation and visible focus states.

title: Implement Route-Based Code Splitting
description: Split JavaScript bundles by route to reduce initial load time and improve performance.

title: Use Descriptive Button Labels
description: Buttons should describe what they do. Avoid generic labels like "Click here" or "Submit".

title: Design for Interruption
description: Users are often interrupted mid-task. Auto-save forms preserve scroll position and draft states reduce frustration.

title: Implement Progressive Web App Features
description: Add PWA capabilities (manifest service worker install prompt) for improved mobile experience.

title: Use Accessible Rich Internet Applications
description: Complex widgets must use ARIA roles properties and states to communicate purpose to assistive technologies.

title: Design for Dark Mode
description: Support prefers-color-scheme media query. Ensure all assets colors and images work in both light and dark modes.

title: Implement Cumulative Layout Shift Prevention
description: Explicitly set image and video dimensions to prevent layout shifts. Target CLS score below 0.1.

title: Use Predictable Navigation Patterns
description: Follow platform conventions for navigation. Users should not need to learn new navigation paradigms.

title: Design Accessible Search
description: Search components need proper ARIA labels live region announcements for results and keyboard navigation.

title: Implement Content Delivery Networks
description: Serve static assets from CDN locations closest to users to reduce latency globally.
